title: What must I do to monitor external corrosion control?
authority: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
content: 49 CFR 195.573 describes monitoring requirements for external corrosion control on hazardous liquid pipelines. Operators must inspect cathodic protection systems, monitor rectifiers and other impressed current power sources, and take prompt remedial action when corrosion control is deficient.

metadata.summary: External corrosion monitoring requirements for hazardous liquid pipelines.

title: Pipeline Safety: Integrity Management Clarifications
authority: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
content: The Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration issued a sample notice discussing integrity management clarification topics, operator recordkeeping, corrosion control programs, and source traceability for compliance review.

metadata.summary: Sample PHMSA filing for toolkit fixture mode.

title: Water Protection
content: 16 TAC § 3.8 covers water protection requirements for oil and gas operations regulated by the Railroad Commission of Texas. The rule addresses pollution prevention, pits, discharges, and operator responsibilities for protecting surface and subsurface water.

metadata.summary: Texas RRC water protection rule sample.

title: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems and Organizations
authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology
content: NIST SP 800-171 provides security requirements for protecting controlled unclassified information in nonfederal systems and organizations. It is commonly used in defense industrial base compliance programs and referenced in contractor cybersecurity obligations.

metadata.summary: Sample government compliance source for non-energy agent integrations.
